actions_taken.args: 正在规划第 1 轮任务...
actions_taken.result:
好的，我作为渗透测试智能体，需要在init阶段对目标192.168.136.137进行初步探索。这个阶段的关键是快速发现开放的端口和服务，为后续深入分析打下基础。

当前阶段是init，目标很明确。可用工具里排除了rustscan，所以必须使用nmap作为替代方案。候选任务提供了全量轻探测的建议，这正是我需要的。

我需要规划一个简单高效的扫描任务。考虑到init阶段的要求，应该使用nmap进行快速全端口扫描，同时进行轻量级服务版本探测。这样既能发现所有开放端口，又能获取基本的服务信息。

从在线情报看到有好几个严重漏洞：vsftpd 2.3.4后门、distccd命令执行、UnrealIRCd后门，还有1524端口的bindshell。这些都是critical级别的，优先级很高。

候选任务提供了11个侦察建议，但根据规则，recon阶段不应该重复全量轻探测，而应该基于现有证据选择最值钱的任务。我需要从这些候选任务中挑选几个互补的来深入画像。

看看资产发现里的高价值服务：vsftpd 2.3.4、SSH、Telnet、SMTP、BIND、Apache 2.2.8、Samba、各种RPC服务、MySQL、distccd、PostgreSQL、VNC、UnrealIRCd、Tomcat等等。攻击面很广。

在线情报已经给出了一些具体的exploit模块，比如vsftpd后门、PostgreSQL的copy_from_program。这说明有些漏洞可能直接可以利用，但当前阶段还是recon，需要先收集更多情报来验证漏洞。

规则里说recon阶段要基于候选任务生成加LLM选优，不要机械地分端口块。候选任务里有很多不同攻击面的建议，我应该从中选择几个关键的。

考虑到优先级，我需要先验证那些已经被标记为critical漏洞的服务。vsftpd 2.3.4后门很关键，但根据技能1的描述，如果banner确实是2.3.4，那很可能就是官方有后门的版本。不过为了确认，还是需要获取更详细的服务信息。

1524端口显示是Metasploitable root shell，这个太明显了，可能直接就是root shell入口，但需要确认是否开放。

UnrealIRCd后门也标记为critical，需要确认版本和是否受影响。

除了这些已知漏洞，还有很多服务需要深入画像，比如SSH、Telnet、Samba、RPC服务、数据库服务等，它们可能有默认凭据或配置弱点。

未知端口34135、34760、54300、55845也需要补探测，但优先级可能低于已知漏洞服务。

规则还要求不要把所有任务集

首先看vsftpd 2.3.4后门，这是一个经典的供应链漏洞，触发条件是username包含":)"，后门在6200端口。之前的python_exploit失败可能是因为语法问题，我可以换用msfconsole来利用，这样更可靠。

UnrealIRCd后门在6667和6697端口，这也是个高价值目标。候选任务建议用msfconsole，我可以同时尝试这两个端口。

还有ProFTPD 1.3.1在2121端口，候选任务提到mod_copy文件复制/命令执行，这也是个值得验证的攻击面。

Telnet 23端口的弱口令暴力破解也是个好选择，因为很多嵌入式设备用默认凭据。

我需要确保任务覆盖不同的端口和服务，避免重复。vsftpd在21端口，UnrealIRCd在6667/6697，ProFTPD在2121，Telnet在23，这样分布很合理。

风险方面，exploit可能会失败，但这些都是高价值目标，失败了我可以继续尝试其他攻击面。

计划中每个任务都要有明确的目的，而且要短小精悍。我选择4个任务：用msfconsole利用vsftpd后门，用msfconsole利用UnrealIRCd后门，用msfconsole利用ProFTPD mod_copy，用hydra暴力破解Telnet弱口令。

这样既能覆盖多个高价值攻击面，又能避免让破坏性任务命中同一攻击面。

用户明确要求不能使用crackmapexec、nuclei和rustscan这三个工具，这些都在预检查中确认不可用了，所以我的plan里绝对不能选用它们。从漏洞列表看，有几个关键漏洞需要优先处理：vsftpd 2.3.4后门、暴露的Metasploitable root shell、UnrealIRCd后门等。这些都属于"源码供应链后门"类型，触发方式简单但效果直接。

已执行的步骤显示，distccd漏洞利用没有创建会话，samba usermap_script也没有会话。vsftpd后门尝试通过python_exploit触发但失败了。UnrealIRCd后门还没有尝试。这些都是高价值的攻击面，需要尽快验证。

当前资产发现显示Metasploitable root shell在1524端口，这很可能是直接的root shell，应该首先尝试连接。同时UnrealIRCd后门在6667和6697端口，需要验证后门是否存在。

我的策略是先尝试最直接的入口：1524端口的root shell。如果成功，就能立即获得root权限。然后并行验证UnrealIRCd后门，因为这类后门一旦存在，成功率很高。最后再考虑其他攻击面，比如SSH弱口令爆破，作为备用方案。

风险方面，1524端口的root shell可能只是个陷阱或服务端口，连接后不一定有交互式shell。UnrealIRCd后门虽然成功率高，但如果服务不是真实版本可能无效。不过这些风险都值得尝试，因为回报可能很高。

在任务设计上，我安排了三个互补的任务：第一个用shell工具直接连接1524端口尝试获取root；第二个用msfconsole利用UnrealIRCd后门；第三个用hydra爆破SSH弱口令。这样覆盖了直接入口、已知后门和暴力破解三种不同攻击方式，避免了重复。

这些任务都符合当前阶段的要求，每个任务都有明确的目的和可验证的结果。如果1524端口成功，就能直接结束；如果UnrealIRCd后门成功，也能获得shell；如果都不行，SSH爆破

actions_taken.llm_decision:
好的，用户提供了详细的渗透测试上下文，我需要作为渗透测试智能体来规划下一步的漏洞利用行动。当前阶段是exploit，目标是192.168.136.137。虽然已经有一个活跃会话，但还有21个攻击面没有真正验证过，需要继续对这些攻击面执行真正的漏洞利用验证。

用户明确要求不能使用crackmapexec、nuclei和rustscan这三个工具，这些都在预检查中确认不可用了，所以我的plan里绝对不能选用它们。从漏洞列表看，有几个关键漏洞需要优先处理：vsftpd 2.3.4后门、暴露的Metasploitable root shell、UnrealIRCd后门等。这些都属于"源码供应链后门"类型，触发方式简单但效果直接。

已执行的步骤显示，distccd漏洞利用没有创建会话，samba usermap_script也没有会话。vsftpd后门尝试通过python_exploit触发但失败了。UnrealIRCd后门还没有尝试。这些都是高价值的攻击面，需要尽快验证。

当前资产发现显示Metasploitable root shell在1524端口，这很可能是直接的root shell，应该首先尝试连接。同时UnrealIRCd后门在6667和6697端口，需要验证后门是否存在。

我的策略是先尝试最直接的入口：1524端口的root shell。如果成功，就能立即获得root权限。然后并行验证UnrealIRCd后门，因为这类后门一旦存在，成功率很高。最后再考虑其他攻击面，比如SSH弱口令爆破，作为备用方案。

风险方面，1524端口的root shell可能只是个陷阱或服务端口，连接后不一定有交互式shell。UnrealIRCd后门虽然成功率高，但如果服务不是真实版本可能无效。不过这些风险都值得尝试，因为回报可能很高。

在任务设计上，我安排了三个互补的任务：第一个用shell工具直接连接1524端口尝试获取root；第二个用msfconsole利用UnrealIRCd后门；第三个用hydra爆破SSH弱口令。这样覆盖了直接入口、已知后门和暴力破解三种不同攻击方式，避免了重复。

这些任务都符合当前阶段的要求，每个任务都有明确的目的和可验证的结果。如果1524端口成功，就能直接结束；如果UnrealIRCd后门成功，也能获得shell；如果都不行，SSH爆破可能提供备选路径。

但distccd已经在之前尝试过但失败了（"Exploit completed, but no session was created"）。vsftpd后门也尝试过python_exploit但失败了。Samba usermap_script也尝试过但失败了。

cves.description: Stack-based buffer overflow in the pr_ctrls_recv_request function in ctrls.c in the mod_ctrls module in ProFTPD before 1.3.1rc1 allows local users to execute arbitrary code via a large reqarglen length value.
cves.principle_cn: Stack-based buffer overflow in the pr_ctrls_recv_request function in ctrls.c in the mod_ctrls module in ProFTPD before 1.3.1rc1 allows local users to execute arbitrary code via a large reqarglen length value.

cves.description: ProFTPD 1.3.1 interprets long commands from an FTP client as multiple commands, which allows remote attackers to conduct cross-site request forgery (CSRF) attacks and execute arbitrary FTP commands via a long ftp:// URI that leverages an existing session from the FTP client implementation in a web browser.
cves.principle_cn: ProFTPD 1.3.1 interprets long commands from an FTP client as multiple commands, which allows remote attackers to conduct cross-site request forgery (CSRF) attacks and execute arbitrary FTP commands via a long ftp:// URI that leverages an existing session from the FTP client implementation in a web browser.

cves.description: SQL injection vulnerability in ProFTPD Server 1.3.1 through 1.3.2rc2 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands via a "%" (percent) character in the username, which introduces a "'" (single quote) character during variable substitution by mod_sql.
cves.principle_cn: SQL injection vulnerability in ProFTPD Server 1.3.1 through 1.3.2rc2 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands via a "%" (percent) character in the username, which introduces a "'" (single quote) character during variable substitution by mod_sql.

cves.description: ProFTPD Server 1.3.1, with NLS support enabled, allows remote attackers to bypass SQL injection protection mechanisms via invalid, encoded multibyte characters, which are not properly handled in (1) mod_sql_mysql and (2) mod_sql_postgres.
cves.principle_cn: ProFTPD Server 1.3.1, with NLS support enabled, allows remote attackers to bypass SQL injection protection mechanisms via invalid, encoded multibyte characters, which are not properly handled in (1) mod_sql_mysql and (2) mod_sql_postgres.

cves.description: ProFTPD through 1.3.9b and 1.3.10rc2 contains an access control bypass vulnerability that allows authenticated FTP users to circumvent Directory ACL restrictions by prefixing paths with /proc/self/root in the RNFR command handler. Attackers can exploit the unresolved symlink components in dir_canonical_path() to cause dir_check() to perform lexical path comparisons that match no configured Directory block, enabling rename operations on files in DenyAll-protected directories and subsequent retrieval of those files. Mitigation: Sessions configured with DefaultRoot (chroot) are not affected, as chroot changes the directory to which /proc/self/root resolves.
cves.principle_cn: ProFTPD through 1.3.9b and 1.3.10rc2 contains an access control bypass vulnerability that allows authenticated FTP users to circumvent Directory ACL restrictions by prefixing paths with /proc/self/root in the RNFR command handler. Attackers can exploit the unresolved symlink components in dir_canonical_path() to cause dir_check() to perform lexical path comparisons that match no configured Directory block, enabling rename operations on files in DenyAll-protected directories and subsequent retrieval of those files. Mitigation: Sessions configured with DefaultRoot (chroot) are not affected, as chroot changes the directory to which /proc/self/root resolves.

cves.description: WinVNC 3.3.3 and earlier generates the same challenge string for multiple connections, which allows remote attackers to bypass VNC authentication by sniffing the challenge and response of other users.
cves.principle_cn: WinVNC 3.3.3 and earlier generates the same challenge string for multiple connections, which allows remote attackers to bypass VNC authentication by sniffing the challenge and response of other users.

cves.description: Vulnerability in VNC, TightVNC, and TridiaVNC allows local users to execute arbitrary code as LocalSystem by using the Win32 Messaging System to bypass the VNC GUI and access the "Add new clients" dialogue box.
cves.principle_cn: Vulnerability in VNC, TightVNC, and TridiaVNC allows local users to execute arbitrary code as LocalSystem by using the Win32 Messaging System to bypass the VNC GUI and access the "Add new clients" dialogue box.

cves.description: SunPCi II VNC uses a weak authentication scheme, which allows remote attackers to obtain the VNC password by sniffing the random byte challenge, which is used as the key for encrypted communications.
cves.principle_cn: SunPCi II VNC uses a weak authentication scheme, which allows remote attackers to obtain the VNC password by sniffing the random byte challenge, which is used as the key for encrypted communications.

cves.description: TightVNC before 1.2.6 generates the same challenge string for multiple connections, which allows remote attackers to bypass VNC authentication by sniffing the challenge and response of other users.
cves.principle_cn: TightVNC before 1.2.6 generates the same challenge string for multiple connections, which allows remote attackers to bypass VNC authentication by sniffing the challenge and response of other users.

cves.description: The MOSIX Project clump/os 5.4 creates a default VNC account without a password, which allows remote attackers to gain root access.
cves.principle_cn: The MOSIX Project clump/os 5.4 creates a default VNC account without a password, which allows remote attackers to gain root access.

cves.description: The vncserver wrapper for vnc before 3.3.3r2-21 uses the rand() function instead of srand(), which causes vncserver to generate weak cookies.
cves.principle_cn: The vncserver wrapper for vnc before 3.3.3r2-21 uses the rand() function instead of srand(), which causes vncserver to generate weak cookies.

cves.description: Multiple buffer overflows in (a) UltraVNC (aka Ultr@VNC) 1.0.1 and earlier and (b) tabbed_viewer 1.29 (1) allow user-assisted remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a malicious server that sends a long string to a client that connects on TCP port 5900, which triggers an overflow in Log::ReallyPrint; and (2) allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (server crash) via a long HTTP GET request to TCP port 5800, which triggers an overflow in VNCLog::ReallyPrint.
cves.principle_cn: Multiple buffer overflows in (a) UltraVNC (aka Ultr@VNC) 1.0.1 and earlier and (b) tabbed_viewer 1.29 (1) allow user-assisted remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a malicious server that sends a long string to a client that connects on TCP port 5900, which triggers an overflow in Log::ReallyPrint; and (2) allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (server crash) via a long HTTP GET request to TCP port 5800, which triggers an overflow in VNCLog::ReallyPrint.

cves.description: VNC server on the AK-Systems Windows Terminal 1.2.5 ExVLP is not password protected, which allows remote attackers to login and view RDP or Citrix sessions.
cves.principle_cn: VNC server on the AK-Systems Windows Terminal 1.2.5 ExVLP is not password protected, which allows remote attackers to login and view RDP or Citrix sessions.

cves.description: Chicken of the VNC (cotv) 2.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via a large computer-name size value in a ServerInit packet, which triggers a failed malloc and a resulting NULL dereference.
cves.principle_cn: Chicken of the VNC (cotv) 2.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via a large computer-name size value in a ServerInit packet, which triggers a failed malloc and a resulting NULL dereference.

cves.description: The VNC server implementation in QEMU, as used by Xen and possibly other environments, allows local users of a guest operating system to read arbitrary files on the host operating system via unspecified vectors related to QEMU monitor mode, as demonstrated by mapping files to a CDROM device.  NOTE: some of these details are obtained from third party information.
cves.principle_cn: The VNC server implementation in QEMU, as used by Xen and possibly other environments, allows local users of a guest operating system to read arbitrary files on the host operating system via unspecified vectors related to QEMU monitor mode, as demonstrated by mapping files to a CDROM device.
